Dear Ms. Bott: 
October 25 
198 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-609 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on October 20, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

sections.question_synthetic: A donor provided checks intended for two candidates; the requestor asks whether the checks can be split between two separate campaign accounts or if they must be returned or handled differently.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC acknowledges receipt of the advice request and indicates it will provide a response within 21 working days for formal written advice, with a note that additional information may be needed to properly advise on the handling of the donor checks.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is heavily degraded/OCR-damaged with significant text corruption making precise extraction difficult. Appears to be a combination of an incoming request letter and FPPC acknowledgment response. The substantive question and conclusion sections are not clearly legible. The extraction captures the general nature of the inquiry based on partially readable fragments.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A donor provided checks intended for two candidates; the requestor asks whether the checks can be split between two separate campaign accounts or if they must be returned or handled differently.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC acknowledges receipt of the advice request and indicates it will provide a response within 21 working days for formal written advice, with a note that additional information may be needed to properly advise on the handling of the donor checks.

Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the information needed. embedding.summary: This is an FPPC acknowledgment letter for advice request 89-609 from Susan Bott regarding the proper handling of donor checks intended for multiple candidates. The FPPC confirms receipt and outlines its response timeline.
